# sheet-cli

> Google Sheets / Drive CLI. Six verbs (`get put del new copy move`) over a
> unified `SID:Sheet!locator` target grammar.

## Quick reference

```
sheet-cli get    TARGET            read cell / range / sheet / spreadsheet / drive
sheet-cli put    TARGET [VALUE]    write cells (scalar sugar, else stdin)
sheet-cli del    TARGET            clear range / delete sheet / row / col / spreadsheet
sheet-cli new    [TARGET]          create spreadsheet / sheet / row / column
sheet-cli copy   SOURCE DEST       copy (server-side when possible)
sheet-cli move   SOURCE DEST       move (server-side when possible)
sheet-cli auth                     run OAuth flow (cache token)
```

## Target grammar

```
SID                       whole spreadsheet
SID:Sheet                 a sheet (by title)
SID:Sheet!A1              a single cell
SID:Sheet!A1:B10          a range
SID:Sheet!5               row 5
SID:Sheet!C               column C
SID:!A1:B10               range in the first/default sheet
<empty>                   Drive (listing)
```

Second operand of `copy` / `move` inherits omitted parts from the first:

```
Sheet!A1                  inherits SID
!A1                       inherits SID + sheet
:Sheet                    inherits SID only
```

Sheet names containing `:`, `!`, spaces, or `'` must be single-quoted:
`SID:'My Sheet'!A1`. Embedded `'` is doubled: `'Bob''s'!A1`.

## Output rules

| verb | default | `--format=json` |
|---|---|---|
| `get` (DRIVE / SPREADSHEET) | JSON | JSON |
| `get` (SHEET / RANGE / ROW / COLUMN) | cell/value text | raw API JSON |
| `put` / `del` / `copy` / `move` | silent | `{target, response}` |
| `new` | always JSON | always JSON |

Exit codes: `0` success, `1` API/auth error, `2` grammar error.
429 / 500 / 503 responses retry automatically (exponential backoff, 3 attempts).

## Input formats (stdin for `put`)

Auto-detected:

**JSON** — cell-keyed OR range-keyed:
```json
{"A1": "hello", "B1": 42, "C1": "=SUM(A1:B1)"}
{"A1:B2": [[1, 2], [3, 4]]}
```

**Cell/value text** — one per line, first space splits cell from value:
```
A1 hello world
A2 123
A3 =SUM(A1:A2)
```

Cell keys without `!` are qualified with the target's sheet. Keys with `!`
pass through unchanged.

## Examples

```bash
# Drive listing
sheet-cli get

# Spreadsheet metadata
sheet-cli get SID

# Read a cell (text)
sheet-cli get SID:Sheet1!A1

# Read a range (raw API)
sheet-cli get SID:Sheet1!A1:C10 --format=json

# Single-cell write
sheet-cli put SID:Sheet1!A1 "hello"

# Batch write from stdin
echo '{"A1": 1, "B1": 2, "C1": "=A1+B1"}' | sheet-cli put SID:Sheet1

# Clear a range
sheet-cli del SID:Sheet1!A1:C10

# Delete a row / column / sheet / spreadsheet
sheet-cli del SID:Sheet1!5
sheet-cli del SID:Sheet1!C
sheet-cli del SID:Sheet1
sheet-cli del SID

# Create
sheet-cli new "My New Spreadsheet"        # echoes new SID + URL
sheet-cli new SID:NewSheet                # add a sheet
sheet-cli new SID:Sheet1!5 --side=above   # insert row above row 5
sheet-cli new SID:Sheet1!C --side=left    # insert col left of C

# Copy / move (server-side when operands line up)
sheet-cli copy SID:Sheet1!A1:B10 :Sheet2!D1       # same-SS → copyPaste
sheet-cli copy SID1:Sheet1 SID2                   # cross-SS sheet → copyTo
sheet-cli copy SID1:Sheet1!A1:B2 SID2:Sheet1!D1   # fallback: read+write
sheet-cli move SID:Sheet1!5 !2                    # moveDimension
sheet-cli move SID:Sheet1!A1:B2 :Sheet2!D1        # cutPaste
```

## Dispatch table (copy / move)

| Source shape | Dest shape | Same SS? | Path |
|---|---|---|---|
| RANGE | RANGE | yes | `copyPaste` (copy) / `cutPaste` (move) — server-side |
| ROW/COL | ROW/COL (same sheet) | yes | `moveDimension` — server-side |
| SHEET | SPREADSHEET | no | `spreadsheets.sheets.copyTo` — server-side |
| RANGE / SHEET | RANGE / SHEET | no | read + write fallback (move also clears source) |

## Bulk writes — recommended pattern for agents

For 10+ cells, generate a JSON object with cell-keyed formulas/values and
pipe to a single `put`. One HTTP round-trip regardless of cell count.

Write a script (avoids shell-heredoc approval prompts):

```python
# /tmp/gen.py
import json
data = {}
for row in range(10, 47):
    data[f"A{row}"] = f"=SUM(B{row}:D{row})"
    data[f"E{row}"] = f"=A{row}*0.027"
print(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
```

Then:

```bash
python3 /tmp/gen.py | venv/bin/sheet-cli put SID:Sheet1
```

Scales to thousands of cells in a single call.

## Authentication

OAuth 2.0 desktop flow. Credentials at `~/.sheet-cli/credentials.json`,
token cached at `~/.sheet-cli/token.pickle`. First run opens a browser;
subsequent runs reuse and auto-refresh. Run `sheet-cli auth` to re-auth or
switch accounts.

## See also

- [mcp-server/README.md](mcp-server/README.md) — same grammar exposed as MCP tools for Claude Desktop
